title: cornus alba f. azurea
intro: Cornus alba f. azurea, also known as the red-osier dogwood, is a deciduous shrub that belongs to the family Cornaceae, characterized by its striking blue-green leaves and vibrant red stems in winter. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and fever. The health benefits of Cornus alba f. azurea include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its bark and roots are often used in traditional folk remedies.
